_comment: SSOT for the headline benchmark numbers quoted in README.md. The docs-consistency CI check (tests/docs/docs-consistency.test.ts) parses the same numbers out of the README prose and asserts they equal these pinned values within tolerance, so README metrics can never silently drift from the pinned truth.
_regenerate: These are the reranked-hybrid headline metrics. To refresh from a real bench run: `npm run bench` (retrieval: MRR / nDCG@5 / Recall@3 on the committed text-embedding-v4 fixture) and `npm run bench:accuracy` (objective figure metrics). Copy the reranked-hybrid row + narrow figure metrics here and update the README prose in the same commit.
_source: README.md reranked-hybrid retrieval result + objective figure-traceability section
_semantic_comment: SSOT for the meaning-level (semantic) self-audit numbers, measured offline by `npm run bench:semantic` over bench/semantic-consistency-dataset.ts with the deterministic FakeJudge. `contradictions_surfaced` is the canonical Problem-Value number quoted verbatim in README.md + BENCHMARK.md; `rule_based_caught` is what a field-level audit / naive store serves as truth on the same corpus. The readiness gate (scripts/readiness.ts) recomputes these and asserts computed == golden == docs.
